text: Fill in the blanks about renal physiology.
questions: The normal glomerular filtration rate (GFR) is approximately *125* mL/min or *180* litres per day.
GFR is determined by the net filtration pressure, which depends on glomerular *hydrostatic* pressure (favouring filtration) and *oncotic/osmotic* pressure (opposing filtration).
The proximal convoluted tubule reabsorbs approximately *65-70* percent of the filtered sodium and water.
ADH (antidiuretic hormone) acts on the *collecting duct* to increase water reabsorption by inserting *aquaporin-2* channels.
The juxtaglomerular apparatus consists of the macula densa, *juxtaglomerular/granular* cells, and extraglomerular mesangial cells, and it regulates *renin* secretion.
